candidates.name: Jeff Landry
candidates.notes: Devout Catholic and strongly conservative governor. Signed Ten Commandments display law for public school classrooms (HB 71, upheld by 5th Circuit 2026). Defended Louisiana's near-total abortion ban as AG. Signed constitutional carry, anti-crime laws, reduced income tax rates, expanded school choice. NRA lifetime member. Trump ally — appointed to Greenland advisory role. Army veteran who served in Desert Storm. As AG, fought Obama/Biden admin on energy, immigration, and religious liberty.

profile.education: University of Louisiana at Lafayette (BS, Environmental & Sustainable Resources); Loyola University New Orleans (JD)
profile.background: Attorney and oil industry worker; U.S. Representative (2011-2013); Louisiana Attorney General (2016-2024); became 57th governor in 2024.

profile.background: Attorney and oil industry worker; U.S. Representative (2011-2013); Louisiana Attorney General (2016-2024); became 57th governor in 2024.

candidates.name: Bill Cassidy
candidates.notes: Senior senator from Louisiana. Physician (gastroenterologist). One of 7 Republican senators who voted to convict Trump in 2nd impeachment (2021) — censured by Louisiana GOP. Voted for Bipartisan Safer Communities Act (gun safety bill 2022), breaking with NRA. Opposes taxpayer-funded healthcare for illegal immigrants. Chairs Senate Finance Committee. Generally conservative on fiscal/healthcare policy but moderate on some social issues. Supports bipartisan infrastructure. Did not endorse Trump initially in 2024 but said he would vote Republican. Heritage 62% reflects moderate-conservative record.

profile.education: Louisiana State University (BS), LSU School of Medicine (MD)
profile.background: Gastroenterologist and associate professor at LSU Health Sciences Center. Co-founded clinic for uninsured. Former Louisiana State Senator and U.S. House member.
profile.office: 455 Dirksen Senate Office Building

claims.text: Voted for Bipartisan Safer Communities Act (gun safety bill 2022), breaking with NRA.

candidates.name: John Kennedy
candidates.notes: Junior senator known for sharp, folksy one-liners at committee hearings. Oxford-educated attorney. Former Democrat who switched to GOP in 2007. Heritage Action scores range from 52% (118th) to 94% (117th), lifetime 77%. Strong 2A supporter (A+ NRA). Pro-life voting record. Opposes sanctuary cities. Supports border wall. Vocal critic of Biden admin spending and judicial nominees. Founding member of his church. Former Louisiana State Treasurer for 17 years.

profile.education: Vanderbilt University (BA), University of Virginia School of Law (JD), Oxford University (BCL)
profile.background: Former Louisiana State Treasurer for 17 years. Attorney and former special counsel to Governor Buddy Roemer. Studied at Oxford. Former Democrat who switched to Republican Party.
profile.office: 437 Russell Senate Office Building
